expertise_area: Mental Health Advocacy and Emergency Medicine

key_insights.insight: Resilience and confidence are distinct qualities; you can be resilient without being outwardly confident.
key_insights.quote: I am confident. That's a good question. No, I'm not. I don't think so.Not inherently confident. I think I would always, if you put me in a party... I'd probably be theguy in the corner quietly chatting to one or two people...but i even though i'm not confident i do think i'm very resilient

key_insights.insight: The fear of a potential negative future is often worse than the reality of the change itself.
key_insights.quote: Your fear when they actually do it they go actually it was fine i wasn't meant to stay in a relationshipyou know i was actually staying in a place of fear and fearing too much rather than going wellactually if you stand to reason you know my current situation is far from perfect i don'twant to be in it why should the next situation be any worse

key_insights.insight: It is crucial to protect the first and last hour of each day by creating boundaries with technology.
key_insights.quote: If I was going to say to anyone about boundaries around using your phone,try and protect the last and first hour of each day... If you pick up your phone you see your ex-girlfriend or you see something or you know trump has said something or it is a new news piece that's really triggering you... you're allowing external factors to dictate the start of your day

key_insights.insight: True success should be defined by the positive impact you have on others, especially in the mental health space.
key_insights.quote: I think it probably would befeeling that I've made a genuine impactin that mental health spaceand that other young people,other people won't take their lifeor will seek helpand get help before they're at that pointor when they're at that point.I think that would be success for me.

key_insights.insight: Introspection and self-questioning are vital to protect yourself from the negative influences from the outside world and society
key_insights.quote: I think anyone listening to this, ask yourself honestly,when is the last time you genuinely sat down and thought about what you want to do in life what actuallyis your purpose what makes you happy what is peace what what what does peace mean to you

key_insights.insight: Sensitivity, often seen as a weakness, can be a strength, enhancing empathy and interpersonal skills.
key_insights.quote: I now I look back and think you knowthe time I thought that was a real weakness but now I see it as a real strength and I think my sensitivity has allowedme to be a better doctor I think my ability to have empathy on my patients

key_insights.insight: The key to a healthy relationship lies in an acceptance of self and imperfections.
key_insights.quote: No one's perfect we're allin the beauty of the world is everyone's imperfect you know we'll all have things that people wouldsay alex that's really annoying trade of yours you know we're gonna have that right it's findingwhat you it's finding is the goodworth the things that you maybe don't think are as perfect about that person

actionable_advice.advice: Regularly question your goals and what drives you to achieve them.
actionable_advice.how_to: Take out a pen and paper and reflect on your goals, direction, and what is preventing you from going in that direction and what you will do to change it.

actionable_advice.advice: Curate your social media feed to reflect your passions and interests.
actionable_advice.how_to: Unfollow or mute accounts that make you feel bad or don't align with your values.

actionable_advice.advice: Protect the first and last hour of your day by establishing boundaries with your phone.
actionable_advice.how_to: Do not use your phone within the first 15-60 minutes of waking up to give yourself the best start for the day and make sure you are not using you phone the last hour of the night to allow your brain the rest it needs.

actionable_advice.advice: Consciously incorporate lifestyle elements into your daily routine
actionable_advice.how_to: Make sure to get plenty of exercise, go outside, find meaningful connections, plan time for friends and family, prioritise good sleep

memorable_stories: The story of Freya, Alex's university friend who passed away from Leukemia, and her advice to him to say yes to opportunities outside of his comfort zone.
Alex's experience working in A&E during the COVID-19 pandemic and the difficult decisions he had to make.
Alex's recounting of receiving the phone call from his father about his brother's suicide.
contrarian_claims: You don't necessarily need to be confident to succeed, resilience and determination are more crucial.
Modern society is inherently broken and designed in a way that goes against how humans are made to be, requiring a complete redesign.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Dr Alex George: My Hardest Day In A&E, Family Suicide & Finding TRUE Purpose
